actions: Read external/README.md lines 220-227 — XCE xce_impact_analysis: accepts changed file paths, returns affected modules + downstream dependencies + risk assessment
Read external/README.md lines 150-189 — Steering rules: 4 of 5 IDEs command 'Use xce_impact_analysis before multi-file changes' at lines 160,171,182
Read handlers/detect-changes.ts (369 lines) — Full handler: readiness gate → diff parse → symbol line-range overlap → affectedSymbols[] + affectedFiles[] only. Zero risk signals.
Read lib/diff-parser.ts (317 lines) — Custom unified-diff parser: extracts file paths + hunk ranges; no risk logic
Read lib/cross-file-edge-resolver.ts (123 lines) — Reconciles import-targeted CALLS edges to concrete definitions only; returns resolved/unresolved/ambiguous stats, not impact analysis
Read code-graph-db.ts:949-987 — queryEdgesFrom/queryEdgesTo: 1-hop edge traversal APIs usable for fan-in/fan-out counts
Read code-graph-db.ts:1017-1037 — queryFileImportDependents: returns (importer, imported) file pairs via IMPORTS edges for downstream blast radius
Read code-graph-db.ts:1039-1083 — queryFileDegrees: file-level connectedness count via UNION ALL for hub centrality signal
Read code-graph-context.ts:458-493 — impact queryMode: walks reverse CALLS+IMPORTS per single symbol anchor, not a changed-file-set synthesized risk payload
Read indexer-types.ts:17-34 — EdgeType includes TESTED_BY (weight 0.6); DEFAULT_EDGE_WEIGHTS provides per-edge-type confidence baselines

label: XCE xce_impact_analysis returns affected modules + downstream dependencies + risk assessment; our detect_changes returns only affected symbols/files without any risk scoring

evidence.quote: xce_impact_analysis: Analyze the impact of changing specific files. Changed files: ['src/auth/middleware.py', 'src/auth/tokens.py'] → Returns: affected modules, downstream dependencies, risk assessment
DetectChangesResult: status, affectedSymbols, affectedFiles, blockedReason, timestamp, readiness — no downstreamDependents, riskScore, or riskAssessment field
return buildResponse({ status: 'ok', affectedSymbols, affectedFiles, timestamp, readiness })

label: cross-file-edge-resolver reconciles import-targeted CALLS edges only — resolved/unresolved/ambiguous counts, not an impact analyzer. No downstream dependency enumeration or risk scoring.

evidence.quote: Reconciles conservative per-file call edges after all scan results have been persisted, so import proxy targets can point at concrete definitions when the global graph has exactly one safe match
WHERE edge.edge_type = 'CALLS' AND target.kind = 'import' — CALLS only, no IMPORTS/TESTED_BY traversal
CrossFileCallResolutionStats: resolved, unresolved, ambiguousSkipped — no risk or impact fields

label: Five deterministic risk signals computable from existing graph: fan-in (queryEdgesTo), fan-out (queryEdgesFrom), hub centrality (queryFileDegrees), test-coverage gap (queryEdgesFrom with TESTED_BY filter), edge confidence (DEFAULT_EDGE_WEIGHTS + metadata.confidence). Transitive import depth also deterministic via BFS.

evidence.quote: queryEdgesTo(symbolId, edgeType?) returns CodeEdgeSourceResult[] — incoming edges; pass 'CALLS'/'IMPORTS' for fan-in count
queryFileDegrees: UNION ALL over source→target + target→source directions per file, returns degree = unique connected file count for hub centrality
queryFileImportDependents: SELECT DISTINCT imported_file_path, importer_file_path FROM code_edges WHERE edge_type = 'IMPORTS' — downstream blast radius at file level

label: Three risk signals require LLM: semantic risk narrative (no graph semantic data), change-intent classification (no change-type signals in graph), criticality weighting per module (no architectural tier labels stored). All are optional enrichment on top of deterministic baseline.

evidence.quote: Returns: affected modules, downstream dependencies, risk assessment — 'risk assessment' implies a score + narrative, which is the LLM-required envelope
DetectorProvenance: 'ast'|'structured'|'regex'|'heuristic'; EdgeEvidenceClass: 'EXTRACTED'|'INFERRED'|'AMBIGUOUS' — structural evidence only, no semantic change-intent labels
Affected symbols computed by line-range overlap only; no semantic diff-content analysis, no change-classification logic

label: Impact analysis risk score formula: normalize(fanIn)*0.35 + normalize(hubDegree)*0.25 + untestedFlag*0.25 + normalize(transitiveDepth)*0.15. All inputs from existing graph APIs. No schema migration.

evidence.quote: queryEdgesFrom + queryEdgesTo: both accept optional edgeType filter, return target/source nodes for counting
queryFileImportDependents + queryFileDegrees: file-level import graph + degree counts, both deterministic SQL aggregations
TESTED_BY weight 0.6 in DEFAULT_EDGE_WEIGHTS; edge metadata stores confidence overrides for edge confidence weighting

label: Estimated LOC for code_graph_impact_analysis: ~333 (250 lib + 80 handler + 3 tool reg). No new deps, no schema migration. Reuses detect_changes output + queryEdgesTo/From + queryFileDegrees + queryFileImportDependents.

evidence.quote: DetectChangesResult exports affectedSymbols[] + affectedFiles[] — can be called internally by impact analysis as step 1
queryEdgesFrom/queryEdgesTo: existing 1-hop APIs cover fan-in, fan-out, test-coverage, edge-confidence lookups
queryFileDegrees: existing degree API covers hub centrality without new SQL
